name: LAS VEGAS SANDS CORP
business: Las Vegas Sands Corp. develops and operates Integrated Resorts featuring premium accommodations, world-class gaming, entertainment, retail malls, convention and exhibition facilities, celebrity chef restaurants, and other amenities. The company owns and operates properties in Macao and Singapore, focusing on the mass market gaming segment, particularly the upscale premium mass, while also catering to VIP patrons.

exposures.sentence: We currently own and operate Integrated Resorts in Macao and Singapore.
During the year ended December 31, 2025, the costs were associated with our evaluation and pursuit of new business opportunities, primarily $193 million for our digital gaming related efforts

catalysts.event: Completion of Phase II of The Londoner Macao
catalysts.sentence: Phase II of The Londoner Macao, which was completed in early 2025, primarily included the conversion of the Sheraton Grand Macao into the Londoner Grand, an upgrade of the gaming areas and the addition of attractions, dining, retail and entertainment offerings.

catalysts.event: Completion of Tower 3 hotel room renovations at Marina Bay Sands
catalysts.sentence: The renovations of the Tower 3 hotel rooms at Marina Bay Sands into world class suites were completed in the second quarter of 2025

catalysts.event: Construction commencement of MBS Expansion Project
catalysts.sentence: Construction works for the project commenced as of May 26, 2025, before the requisite commencement date under the Second Supplemental Agreement.

catalysts.event: Anticipated opening of MBS Expansion Project
catalysts.sentence: While our current estimate is that construction will be complete by June 2030 with an anticipated opening date in January 2031
These investments must be accomplished by December 2032.

tamClaims.claim: Macao annual gross gaming revenues were 247.40 billion patacas (approximately $30.87 billion) in 2025, up 9.1% year-over-year.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Macao government statistics issued publicly on a monthly basis by the Gaming Inspection and Coordination Bureau (commonly referred to as the DICJ ), annual gross gaming revenues were 247.40 billion patacas in 2025 (approximately $30.87 billion at exchange rates in effect on December 31, 2025), an increase of 9.1% compared to 2024.

tamClaims.claim: Macao visitation was approximately 40 million in 2025, up 14.7% year-over-year.
tamClaims.sentence: Visitation to Macao was approximately 40 million in 2025, an increase of 14.7% compared to 2024.

tamClaims.claim: Singapore welcomed approximately 16.9 million international visitors in 2025, up 2.3% year-over-year.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on figures released by the Singapore Tourism Board (the STB ), Singapore welcomed approximately 16.9 million international visitors during the year ended December 31, 2025, a 2.3% increase compared to 2024.

tamClaims.claim: Singapore tourism receipts were estimated at 29.78 billion SGD (approximately $23.18 billion) in 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: Tourism receipts were estimated to be 29.78 billion Singapore dollars ( SGD, approximately $23.18 billion at exchange rates in effect on December 31, 2025) in 2024 (the latest information publicly available at the time of filing).
